Off Calendar Jan-23-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jan-15-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, 3689.

David H. Williams, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation.
Christopher Crow, an investigator for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant National Entertainment Collectibles Association, Inc.
Default was entered against the above-mentioned defendant on September 26, 2008.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, January 15, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation, and against the Defaulted Defendant National Entertainment Collectibles Association, Inc. as follows:

Civil Penalties pursuant to Heal & Safety Code Section 25249.7(b) - $40,000.00.
Attorney's Fees & Costs - $38,196.50, for a total judgment in the amount of $78,196.50.

Additionally, defendant National Entertainment Collectibles Association, Inc. is enjoined from offering string light products as set forth Paragraph 1 of the Judgment.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jan-06-2009 Continued To Jan-15-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Commissioner: Bruce E. Chan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: L. Jones.
David H. Williams, Esq. for Plaintiff: Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation.
Plaintiff's Witness Christopher Crow is also present.
At Mr. Williams's request, matter is continued to Jan. 15, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., for Plaintiff to dismissed the non-defaulted defendants pursuant to the settlement agreements by the parties.
